title: AGENTS for Rggml
text: Rggml carrier package: vendors CPU backend GGML tensor library static library, installs headers, exposes GGML’s core tensor-context matrix-multiply compute path R_RegisterCCallable() C-callable entry points. high-level R modeling API . R surface ggml_version() plus internal smoke-test helper. Downstream packages LinkingTo: Rggml drive GGML C/C++ code. vendored subset CPU-architecture-generic (-DGGML_CPU_GENERIC): correctness/portability raw speed. SIMD flags, OpenMP, Vulkan/CUDA/Metal/BLAS backends.

what: Vendored GGML Tensor Library with C-Callable Compute API
title: Vendored GGML Tensor Library with C-Callable Compute API
text: Rggml carrier package: vendors CPU backend GGML tensor library static library, installs headers, exposes GGML’s core tensor-context matrix-multiply compute path R_RegisterCCallable() C-callable entry points. modeling ops API - R-level surface ggml_version() internal smoke-test helper. point package R packages link drive GGML tensor math C/C++ code, without vendoring GGML .

title: Vendored GGML Tensor Library with C-Callable Compute API
text: CPU-, architecture-generic subset GGML (~5,100 lines across ~35 vendored .c/.cpp/.h files, see inst/COPYRIGHTS exact file list provenance): tensor/context/graph core, backend abstraction, tensor-level quantize/dequantize (including K-quants Q4_K), CPU backend’s compute kernels, BLAS backend. CPU kernels built -DGGML_CPU_GENERIC (portable scalar/reference path - -march SIMD flags, OpenMP, hand-written x86 SIMD kernel files). dense matrix products run scalar: GGML’s BLAS backend (-DGGML_USE_BLAS) offloads F32 mul_mat whatever BLAS R build links (reference libRblas, OpenBLAS, MKL, Accelerate, …) - BLAS universal R, needs extra system dependency. GGML’s BLAS backend written C cblas_sgemm interface, R guarantee (R guarantees Fortran sgemm_); Rggml bridges gap small portable shim (inst/ggml/cblas.h + rggml_cblas.c) forwards cblas_sgemm Fortran sgemm_ via R’s F77_NAME() convention, linking $(BLAS_LIBS) $(FLIBS). quantized dequant-dot path BLAS cover scalar either: hot q4_K x q8_K vec-dot compiled configure ISA-specific variants - -mavx2 -mfma -O3 x86 (including Intel macOS) -O3 NEON aarch64 (Apple Silicon, ARM Linux/Graviton, Windows--ARM) - CPUID dispatcher (tools/simd/) picks best runtime, falling back GGML’s scalar reference. follows RsimdDispatch strategy: ISA flags live configure, never R’s recorded package flags, R CMD check raises “non-portable flags” NOTE, variant ever called ISA confirmed runtime (single ., dlopen). x86 AVX2 q4_K dot measures ~6-7x faster scalar reference. kernels (q6_K, q8_0) hand-tuned intrinsics next steps. (GGML’s GGML_CPU_ALL_VARIANTS deliberately used: requires GGML_BACKEND_DL + separate per-variant shared libraries loaded via dlopen, fit single-.CRAN package.) GGML tree generated, mystery copy: tools/vendor-ggml/ monorepo regenerates SHA-pinned ggmlR CRAN tarball plus patches overlay files, CI job asserts committed tree still equals recipe. ggmlR carries CRAN-compliance adaptations (stdio/abort redirection) GGML’s C code needs run inside R. See inst/COPYRIGHTS tools/vendor-ggml/PROVENANCE.md attribution, patch list update policy (GGML ggmlR MIT; Rggml’s code GPL (>= 2)).

title: Vendored GGML Tensor Library with C-Callable Compute API
text: GGML’s Vulkan backend vendored builds request, vendor’s GPU (NVIDIA, AMD, Intel): needs glslc Vulkan headers build time (libvulkan-dev + glslc Debian/Ubuntu; Windows LunarG Vulkan SDK VULKAN_SDK set), Vulkan driver run time. deliberately auto-detected. GGML embeds shaders compiled SPIR-V: mul_mm.comp 464-line GLSL template shader generator compiles 1,214 variants (every dtype × alignment × accumulator × cooperative-matrix combination), emitted one C array per variant. resulting mul_mm.comp.cpp 141 MB brace-initializer costs ~5 GB RAM compile - identical -O0, cost compiler parsing literal. Enabling machine happens glslc installed ambush CRAN builders; without flag, nothing build changes. rggml_vulkan_info() reports devices GGML can see, build without backend reports zero devices rather failing, callers can probe fall back. GPU backend’s tensors must live device memory, Rggml_backend_alloc_ctx_tensors/tensor_set/tensor_get C-callables ; path backend-agnostic, code drives CPU, BLAS Vulkan. Correctness pinned CPU backend can exercised without GPU Mesa’s software driver, since GGML_VK_ALLOW_CPU=1 opts CPU-type Vulkan devices upstream refuses. CUDA Metal built.

title: Vendored GGML Tensor Library with C-Callable Compute API
text: Declared installed header inst/include/Rggml.h, registered src/rggml_init.c, implemented src/rggml_api.c: Every one <Name>_ptr() accessor Rggml.h resolves via R_GetCCallable() returns function pointer, e.g. Rggml_context_create_ptr() returns Rggml_context_create_fun.
code: /* version / identity */ int            Rggml_api_version(void); const char    *Rggml_version(void);  /* context lifecycle */ struct ggml_context *Rggml_context_create(size_t mem_size, int no_alloc); void                  Rggml_context_free(struct ggml_context *ctx); size_t                Rggml_used_mem(const struct ggml_context *ctx); size_t                Rggml_tensor_overhead(void); size_t                Rggml_graph_overhead(size_t size);  /* tensor creation / introspection (zero-copy: pass a non-NULL `data`) */ struct ggml_tensor *Rggml_new_tensor(struct ggml_context *ctx, enum ggml_type type,                                       int n_dims, const int64_t *ne, void *data); void    *Rggml_tensor_data(const struct ggml_tensor *tensor); void     Rggml_tensor_set_data(struct ggml_tensor *tensor, void *data); int64_t  Rggml_tensor_ne(const struct ggml_tensor *tensor, int dim); size_t   Rggml_tensor_nb(const struct ggml_tensor *tensor, int dim);  /* CPU backend */ ggml_backend_t Rggml_backend_cpu_init(void); void            Rggml_backend_free(ggml_backend_t backend); int             Rggml_backend_graph_compute(ggml_backend_t backend, struct ggml_cgraph *cgraph);  /* BLAS backend (dense F32 mul_mat via R's BLAS; drop-in `backend` above) */ ggml_backend_t Rggml_backend_blas_init(void); void            Rggml_backend_blas_set_n_threads(ggml_backend_t backend_blas, int n_threads);  /* graph building */ struct ggml_cgraph *Rggml_new_graph(struct ggml_context *ctx, size_t size); void                 Rggml_build_forward_expand(struct ggml_cgraph *cgraph, struct ggml_tensor *tensor);  /* matrix multiply */ struct ggml_tensor *Rggml_mul_mat(struct ggml_context *ctx, struct ggml_tensor *a, struct ggml_tensor *b); int Rggml_compute_mul_mat(struct ggml_context *ctx, ggml_backend_t backend,                            struct ggml_tensor *a, struct ggml_tensor *b,                            float *out_f32, double *out_f64);  /* quantize / dequantize (GGUF-byte-compatible payloads; dequantize is GGML's  * authoritative type-traits to_float reference decoder) */ size_t Rggml_quantize(enum ggml_type type, const float *src, void *dst,                       int64_t nrows, int64_t n_per_row); int    Rggml_dequantize(enum ggml_type type, const void *src, float *dst, int64_t n);  /* type/size introspection */ size_t       Rggml_type_size(enum ggml_type type); size_t       Rggml_row_size(enum ggml_type type, int64_t ne); int64_t      Rggml_blck_size(enum ggml_type type); size_t       Rggml_nbytes(const struct ggml_tensor *tensor); int64_t      Rggml_nelements(const struct ggml_tensor *tensor); const char  *Rggml_type_name(enum ggml_type type);

what: The ggml_mul_mat() convention (verified, not just asserted)
title: Vendored GGML Tensor Library with C-Callable Compute API
text: load R matrix directly GGML tensor ne = c(nrow(M), ncol(M)) pointing M’s raw column-major memory (copy, transpose - exactly zero-copy path Rggml_new_tensor(..., data) ), : result, read back way (dim c(ncol(), ncol(B))), always GGML_TYPE_F32 regardless input types. exercised inst/tinytest/test_mul_mat.R hand-computed small cases larger random matrices, ggml-managed zero-copy tensor paths, driven entirely registered C-callables (via .Call() src/rggml_test.c, uses Rggml.h/R_GetCCallable() path) - path downstream LinkingTo package use.
code: ggml_mul_mat(ctx, A, B)  ==  crossprod(A, B)  ==  t(A) %*% B

what: How a downstream package uses it
title: Vendored GGML Tensor Library with C-Callable Compute API
text: GGML re-vendoring, linking Rggml.linker level - R_GetCCallable() symbol resolution run time (standard R C-callable convention, e.g. Rfmalloc/Rgguf).
code: # DESCRIPTION Imports: Rggml LinkingTo: Rggml /* your_package_file.c */ #include <Rggml.h>   /* pulls in ggml.h, ggml-alloc.h, ggml-backend.h, ggml-cpu.h too */  void do_something(void) {     Rggml_context_create_fun ctx_create = Rggml_context_create_ptr();     Rggml_backend_cpu_init_fun backend_init = Rggml_backend_cpu_init_ptr();     Rggml_compute_mul_mat_fun compute_mul_mat = Rggml_compute_mul_mat_ptr();     /* ... build tensors with struct ggml_tensor *, run ggml_mul_mat, ... */ }

title: Vendored GGML Tensor Library with C-Callable Compute API
text: R CMD check - clean (Status: OK, 0 warnings/notes) Linux; cross-platform matrix (Linux + macOS) runs CI, see badge . tinytest::test_package("Rggml") - tests pass, including end--end ggml_mul_mat() proof . Platforms: Linux, macOS Windows. Windows build goes configure.win, re-execs one configure RGGML_WINDOWS=1 SIMD probe, Vulkan shader pipeline libggml.build duplicated; writes src/Makevars.win (-ldl - Windows libdl) finds Vulkan SDK Windows layout ($VULKAN_SDK/Bin/glslc.exe, -lvulkan-1). Verified windows-latest job CI.

what: Rggml: Vendored 'GGML' Tensor Library with C-Callable Compute API — Rggml-package
title: Rggml: Vendored 'GGML' Tensor Library with C-Callable Compute API — Rggml-package
text: Rggml carrier package: vendors CPU backend 'GGML' tensor library (https://github.com/ggml-org/ggml) static library, installs headers, exposes 'GGML' tensor-context matrix-multiply compute R_RegisterCCallable() entry points. high-level R modeling API beyond ggml_version.

title: Rggml: Vendored 'GGML' Tensor Library with C-Callable Compute API — Rggml-package
text: Add Rggml LinkingTo (Imports, namespace guaranteed loaded first) package's DESCRIPTION, #include <Rggml.h> C/C++ source. header also pulls vendored public 'GGML' headers (ggml.h, ggml-alloc.h, ggml-backend.h, ggml-cpu.h), real 'GGML' types (struct ggml_context *, struct ggml_tensor *, ggml_backend_t, ...) available directly - re-vendoring 'GGML' linking Rggml's shared object required. Every wrapped function <Name>_ptr() accessor resolves symbol via R_GetCCallable() first time needed.

title: Rggml: Vendored 'GGML' Tensor Library with C-Callable Compute API — Rggml-package
text: CPU backend built, using GGML's architecture-generic (non-SIMD) reference kernels maximum portability across CRAN build machines. See README.md future 'Vulkan' backend require.

text: exported; quantized analogue rggml_test_mul_mat() exact operation Rfmalloc typed-GEMM bridge performs. weight matrix quantized Q4_K heap buffer standing mmap'd 'GGUF' payload, wrapped zero-copy Q4_K tensor, multiplied dense F32 activations B. ggml_mul_mat() contracts Q4_K weight row B's columns runtime-SIMD-dispatched ggml_vec_dot_q4_K_q8_K (AVX2/NEON staged), quantizing B Q8_K fly inference.

title: Vulkan GPU backend availability — rggml_vulkan_info
text: Reports Vulkan devices GGML can see. Rggml contains Vulkan backend built - opt-, generating compiling GGML's 156 embedded SPIR-V shaders expensive (largest one needs several GB RAM):

title: Vulkan GPU backend availability — rggml_vulkan_info
Vulkan GPU backend availability — rggml_vulkan_info
text: list n_devices (integer) device (description device 0, NA none). rggml_has_vulkan() returns TRUE least one Vulkan device usable.

title: Vulkan GPU backend availability — rggml_vulkan_info
text: also requires glslc Vulkan headers build time (libvulkan-dev + glslc Debian/Ubuntu, LunarG Vulkan SDK VULKAN_SDK set), Vulkan driver run time. software driver Mesa's lavapipe counts: slow, makes backend testable without GPU. Rggml built without Vulkan, returns zero devices rather failing, callers can probe fall back.

title: Vulkan GPU backend availability — rggml_vulkan_info
Rggml 0.1.0 (unreleased)
text: aarch64, GGML’s hand-tuned NEON kernels compiled instead portable scalar reference: 23 vec_dots covering every quantized type, just one q4_K variant SIMD dispatcher stages. NEON mandatory baseline architecture, ggml-cpu/arch/arm/quants.c needs ISA flag produce binary run machine built ; configure therefore drops -DGGML_CPU_GENERIC skips dispatcher (arch/arm defines canonical symbols duplicate). x86 keeps dispatcher purpose: arch/x86’s kernels selected compile-time #defined(__AVX2__), -mavx2 build require AVX2 runtime - GGML upstream needs GGML_CPU_ALL_VARIANTS plus dlopen . Fixed BLAS link failure Windows really portability bug. R guarantees double-precision BLAS: <R_ext/BLAS.h> declares single-precision routine Rblas.dll exports sgemm_. Linux reference BLAS builds happen export , cblas_sgemm shim linked . configure now link-probes sgemm_ R’s BLAS_LIBS/FLIBS; missing, shim promotes operands double, calls dgemm_ (still optimized BLAS), demotes result - correct float precision, naive triple loop temporaries allocated. Set RGGML_NO_SGEMM=1 force promoted path anywhere. Rggml now builds Windows (Rtools/MinGW); OS_type: unix gone. R runs configure.win, re-execs single configure RGGML_WINDOWS=1 rather duplicating SIMD probe, Vulkan shader pipeline libggml.build. Windows branch writes src/Makevars.win without -ldl (Windows libdl) locates Vulkan SDK Windows layout ($VULKAN_SDK/Bin/glslc.exe, -lvulkan-1). windows-latest CI job verifies . vendored GGML already Windows-hardened: -pointer ggml_backend_buffer_i never-destroyed-teardown-singleton patches exist precisely passing POD value, running destructors exit, crashed Windows/MinGW. Added Vulkan GPU backend (API version 7), vendored pinned ggmlR tarball CPU core (version-matches ) tools/vendor-ggml. opt-build time, never auto-detected: Auto-detection hostile: GGML embeds 156 compiled SPIR-V shaders C++ translation units, largest (mul_mm, 141 MB array literal) needs ~5 GB RAM compile - independent -O level, since cost parsing literal. configure probes glslc’s optional shader features (coopmat, coopmat2, integer_dot, bfloat16), builds shader generator, compiles shader set features imply; errors clearly ---vulkan given without glslc/Vulkan headers. Without flag, nothing build changes. New C-callables: Rggml_backend_vulkan_init, Rggml_backend_vulkan_device_count, Rggml_backend_vulkan_device_description; report zero devices instead failing backend built, callers can probe fall back. R-level: rggml_vulkan_info(), rggml_has_vulkan(). Added device-buffer residency C-callables (API version 7): Rggml_backend_alloc_ctx_tensors, Rggml_backend_buffer_free, Rggml_backend_tensor_set, Rggml_backend_tensor_get. CPU BLAS backends compute host memory, pointing tensor R buffer works; GPU backend’s tensors must live device memory. wrap GGML’s backend-agnostic path - allocate no_alloc context’s tensors one backend buffer, upload, compute, download - identical CPU, BLAS Vulkan, test_vulkan.R uses assert GPU backend computes product CPU one. Added view copy C-callables (API version 6): Rggml_view_1d/ _2d/_3d (byte offsets/strides, ggml) Rggml_cpy - building blocks KV cache: cpy nodes write new keys/values views persistent cache tensor, expanded graph ahead attention nodes read views cache (see Rllm’s incremental decoding). Added transformer graph-op C-callables (API version 5): ops forward pass composes - Rggml_get_rows, Rggml_rms_norm, Rggml_mul, Rggml_add, Rggml_silu, Rggml_scale, Rggml_soft_max, Rggml_diag_mask_inf, Rggml_rope (wrapping ggml_rope_ext YaRN ), shape ops Rggml_reshape_2d/_3d, Rggml_permute, Rggml_cont, Rggml_transpose. Downstream packages can now assemble compute full transformer graphs (see Rllm’s llama forward pass) without linking GGML. Added quantization C-callables (API version 4): Rggml_quantize wraps ggml_quantize_chunk() downstream packages can encode f32 rows GGUF block format (output byte-compatible GGUF tensor payloads), Rggml_dequantize wraps GGML’s type-traits to_float - authoritative reference dequantizer, used Rfmalloc ecosystem cross-validate codec decoders (cross-validation caught pinned Q4_K decode bug Rgguf’s vendored gguflib; see package’s NEWS). Verified full quantized-weight compute path external payload: Q4_K tensor pointed zero-copy heap buffer standing mmap’d GGUF payload, multiplied dense F32 activations via ggml_mul_mat() CPU backend, routes weight row runtime-SIMD-dispatched vec_dot (activations quantized Q8_K fly, llama.cpp inference) tracks true product q4_K accuracy (test_mul_mat_q4k.R). Initial release. Rggml carrier package: vendors CPU-, architecture-generic build ‘GGML’ tensor library static library (inst/ggml/libggml.), installs headers, exposes GGML’s tensor-context/matrix-multiply compute path R_RegisterCCallable C-callable entry points, declared downstream use inst/include/Rggml.h. Registered C-callables: context lifecycle (Rggml_context_create, Rggml_context_free, plus Rggml_used_mem/Rggml_tensor_overhead/ Rggml_graph_overhead sizing), tensor creation optional zero-copy external data pointer (Rggml_new_tensor, Rggml_tensor_data, Rggml_tensor_set_data, Rggml_tensor_ne, Rggml_tensor_nb), CPU backend (Rggml_backend_cpu_init, Rggml_backend_free, Rggml_backend_graph_compute), graph building (Rggml_new_graph, Rggml_build_forward_expand), matrix multiply (Rggml_mul_mat, canned single-op Rggml_compute_mul_mat also copies F32 result caller-provided float*/double* buffer), type/size introspection (Rggml_type_size, Rggml_row_size, Rggml_blck_size, Rggml_nbytes, Rggml_nelements, Rggml_type_name). Added runtime SIMD dispatch GGML’s hot quantized vec-dot kernels, starting q4_K x q8_K. configure compiles kernel ISA-specific variants (-mavx2 -mfma -O3 x86 incl. Intel macOS; -O3 NEON aarch64 incl. Apple Silicon) staged tools/simd/, CPUID dispatcher (tools/simd/rggml_simd_dispatch.c, using vendored copy RsimdDispatch’s cpu_features) selects best runtime, falling back GGML’s scalar reference. ISA flags live configure, R’s recorded package flags, R CMD check raises non-portable-flags NOTE; variant called ISA confirmed runtime (single ., dlopen, unlike GGML’s GGML_CPU_ALL_VARIANTS). AVX2 q4_K dot measures ~6-7x faster scalar reference x86. Enabled GGML’s BLAS backend (Rggml_backend_blas_init, Rggml_backend_blas_set_n_threads): dense F32 mul_mat offloads whatever BLAS R build links , since BLAS universal R. GGML’s backend calls C cblas_sgemm, R provide, Rggml bridges small portable shim (inst/ggml/cblas.h + rggml_cblas.c) forwarding Fortran BLAS via F77_NAME(), linking $(BLAS_LIBS) $(FLIBS). BLAS backend drop-backend existing Rggml_compute_mul_mat/Rggml_backend_graph_compute path. Added ggml_version(), returning vendored GGML library’s runtime version string. Verified documented ggml_mul_mat() convention: loading two R matrices directly GGML tensors ne = dim(matrix) (raw column-major bytes, copy transpose), ggml_mul_mat(ctx, , B) produces result tensor dim (ncol(), ncol(B)) equal crossprod(, B) (t() %*% B). Covered tinytest suite hand-checkable small cases larger random matrices, ggml-managed zero-copy (externally-owned buffer) tensor creation paths, exercised via registered C-callables (internal implementation functions directly). CPU kernels architecture-generic (-march SIMD flags, OpenMP, hand-written x86 SIMD kernel files) CRAN-facing build portability, plus BLAS backend dense products. Vulkan/CUDA/Metal GPU backends. Runtime-SIMD-dispatched quantized kernels (GGML_CPU_ALL_VARIANTS) Vulkan backend planned; see README.md.
